children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in this Act, any amendment made by this Act, the Communications Act of 1934 ( et seq.), or any Commission regulation or order may prevent the imposition and collection of a fee or charge applicable to a commercial mobile service or an IP-enabled voice service specifically designated by a State, a political subdivision of a State, an Indian Tribe, or village or regional corporation serving a region established pursuant to the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act ( et seq.) for 9–8–8 related services, if the fee or charge is held in a sequestered account to be obligated or expended only in support of 9–8–8 services, or enhancements of such services, as specified in the provision of State or local law adopting the fee or charge.47 U.S.C. 15143 U.S.C. 1601

children.label: A
children.text: ensuring the efficient and effective routing of calls made to the 9–8–8 national suicide prevention and mental health crisis hotline to an appropriate crisis center; and

children.label: B
children.text: personnel and the provision of acute mental health, crisis outreach and stabilization services by directly responding to the 9–8–8 national suicide prevention and mental health crisis hotline.

details the status in each State, political subdivision of a State, Indian Tribe, or village or regional corporation serving a region established pursuant to the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act ( et seq.) of the collection and distribution of such fees or charges; and43 U.S.C. 1601

includes findings on the amount of revenues obligated or expended by each State, political subdivision of a State, Indian Tribe, or village or regional corporation serving a region established pursuant to the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act ( et seq.) for any purpose other than the purpose for which any such fees or charges are specified.43 U.S.C. 1601

children.heading: Commercial mobile service
children.label: 1 Commercial mobile service
children.text: The term “commercial mobile service” has the meaning given that term under section 332(d) of the Communications Act of 1934 ().47 U.S.C. 332(d)

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 2 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Federal Communications Commission.

children.label: A
children.text: an interconnected VoIP service, as defined in section 9.3 of the title 47 of the Code of Federal Regulations, or any successor thereto; and1So in original.1

children.heading: State
children.label: 4 State
children.text: The term “State” has the meaning given that term in .section 615b of this title
